hypothesis: If metal superhydride discovery is structured around chemical concepts of electron localization at interstitial quasi-atom sites in metal lattices, then a machine learning workflow trained on these chemically meaningful structural features will more efficiently identify thermodynamically stable and superconducting superhydrides than unguided high-throughput searches, because constraining the search space to physically motivated metal lattice configurations reduces combinatorial complexity while preserving the key structural determinants of stability and high-temperature superconductivity.
